input.background: Refer to Example 5.1 and the outcome $x$ of a $\$ 5$ wager in craps. We found the sampling distributions of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ and the sample median $m$ for random samples of $n=3$ dice rolls from a population defined by the following probability distribution: \begin{tabular}{l|ccc} \hline $\boldsymbol{x}$ & -5 & 0 & 5 \\ \hline $\boldsymbol{p}(\boldsymbol{x})$ & $\frac{1}{9}$ & $\frac{6}{9}$ & $\frac{2}{9}$ \\ \hline \end{tabular} The sampling distributions of $\bar{x}$ and $m$ were found to be as follows: \begin{tabular}{l|ccccccc} \hline $\boldsymbol{x}$ & -5 & -3.33 & -1.67 & 0 & 1.67 & 3.33 & 5 \\ \hline $\boldsymbol{p}(\overline{\boldsymbol{x}})$ & $\frac{1}{729}$ & $\frac{18}{729}$ & $\frac{114}{729}$ & $\frac{288}{729}$ & $\frac{228}{729}$ & $\frac{72}{729}$ & $\frac{8}{729}$ \\ \hline \end{tabular} \begin{tabular}{l|ccc} \hline $\boldsymbol{m}$ & -5 & 0 & 5 \\ \hline $\boldsymbol{p}(\boldsymbol{m})$ & $\frac{25}{729}$ & $\frac{612}{729}$ & $\frac{92}{729}$ \\ \hline \end{tabular}
input.question: Show that $m$ is a biased estimator of $\mu$ in this situation.

output.answer: The expected value of the sample median $m$ is $$ E(m)=\Sigma m p(m)=(-5)\left(\frac{25}{729}\right)+(0)\left(\frac{612}{729}\right)+(5)\left(\frac{92}{729}\right)=\frac{335}{729}=.460 $$ Since the expected value of $m$ is not equal to $\mu(\mu=.556)$, the sample median $m$ is a biased estimator of $\mu$.

meta_info.caption: Biased and Unbiased Estimators-Craps Application
meta_info.section: 5.2 Properties of Sampling Distributions: Unbiasedness and Minimum Variance
